title: ～がい
explanation: Expresses 'worth doing' for verbs. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The ～がい grammar point is used to express that an action is worth doing or to emphasize the value and importance of doing something. It is attached to verbs in their stem form and can be translated as 'worth doing' in English.

title: ～かいがあって
titleEn: Used to express that an effort is worth it or has value; 'to be worth the effort' or 'to pay off'.
structure: Verb-past + かいがあって / Noun + の + かいがあって
structureEn: Verb-past + かいがあって / Noun + の + かいがあって
explanation: Used to express that an effort is worth it or has value; 'to be worth the effort' or 'to pay off'. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The ～かいがあって grammar point is used to convey the idea that an action or effort is worth it, or that it has value. It is often translated as 'to be worth the effort' or 'to pay off' in English. It is typically associated with actions or efforts that lead to a successful or desirable result.

title: ～かいもなく
titleEn: Expresses the meaning of 'without worth', 'in vain', 'fruitless'.
structure: Verb-た + かいもなく / Noun + の + かいもなく
structureEn: Verb-た + かいもなく / Noun + の + かいもなく
explanation: Expresses the meaning of 'without worth', 'in vain', 'fruitless'. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The ～かいもなく grammar point is used to express that something has been done without worth, in vain, or fruitlessly. It often appears as 'Verb-た + かいもなく' or 'Noun + の + かいもなく' to indicate that an effort did not produce the hoped-for result.

title: ～かける
titleEn: Indicates a process or action is 'in progress' or 'halfway'.
explanation: Indicates a process or action is 'in progress' or 'halfway'. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The ～かける grammar point is used to describe a process or action that is in progress, halfway, or not yet completed. It can be attached to verbs to imply that the action is still ongoing or something is in a certain state of partial completion.

title: ～がち
titleEn: Expresses that something tends to occur frequently or habitually.
structure: Verb-ます stem + がち / い-Adjective stem + がち / な-Adjective stem + がち
structureEn: Verb-ます stem + がち / い-Adjective stem + がち / な-Adjective stem + がち
explanation: Expresses that something tends to occur frequently or habitually. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The ～がち grammar point is used to express that something has a tendency to occur frequently or habitually. It can be attached to verbs and adjectives to indicate that the action or state tends to occur often.

title: ～か～ないかのうちに
titleEn: Expresses an action that occurs immediately after another action; 'as soon as', 'no sooner than', 'the moment'.
structure: Verb-casual + か + Verb-negative form + かのうちに
structureEn: Verb-casual + か + Verb-negative form + かのうちに
explanation: Expresses an action that occurs immediately after another action; 'as soon as', 'no sooner than', 'the moment'. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The ～か～ないかのうちに grammar point is used to express that an action occurs immediately after another action. In English, it can be translated as 'as soon as', 'no sooner than', or 'the moment'. This grammar point is used with verbs, showing the verb in both affirmative and negative forms with か and ないか, followed by のうちに.

title: ～かねない
titleEn: Express the likelihood of a negative outcome or consequence.
explanation: Express the likelihood of a negative outcome or consequence. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The ～かねない grammar point is used to express the possibility or likelihood of a negative outcome or consequence, often used when there is concern about the result. It is typically used with verbs in their stem form and can be understood as 'might (have a bad result)'.

title: ～かねる
titleEn: Express inability or impossibility of doing an action (often as a polite refusal).
explanation: Express inability or impossibility of doing an action (often as a polite refusal). (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The ～かねる grammar point is used to express that someone cannot do an action or that something is impossible to do. It typically has a nuance of reluctance or polite refusal and is formed by adding かねる to the ます-stem of the verb.
